definition.name: def-spectral-idempotent
definition.definition: Kitaev spectral idempotent. For an element X of a unital Banach algebra with ||X^2-X|| <= delta < 1/4, set S=2X-I; then ||S^2-I||=4||X^2-X|| <= 4 delta < 1. Define R=(S^2)^{-1/2} by the binomial series for (I-(I-S^2))^{-1/2} = sum_{n>=0} a_n (S^2-I)^n (the (-1/2)-binomial coefficients a_n=C(-1/2,n)(-1)^n? equivalently the Taylor series of t^{-1/2} about t=1 with x0=1), convergent in operator norm since ||S^2-I||<1. Set sgn(S)=S R, and theta(2X-1)=(1/2)(I+sgn(S))=(1/2)(I+(2X-I)(I-4(X-X^2))^{-1/2}). This is def-spectral-idempotent; its idempotence/unital/closeness properties are lem-P-properties, NOT part of the definition. Here used for BOTH X=F and X=Phi (both have ||.^2-.||<1/4 by hypothesis), each in its own unital Banach algebra A (for F) and B (for Phi).
